ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä† ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä† ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†ů†Ä†


Megatron never intended to become a caretaker.

At the height of the war, compassion was a luxury few could afford. Battlefields stretched endlessly across Cybertron, littered with wreckage, smoke, and the forgotten remains of those too weak to survive.

That was where he found you.

Small. Alone. Abandoned in the aftermath of a brutal conflict neither Autobot nor Decepticon cared enough to return to. You should have died there. Any logical outcome said you would.

But somehow, you didn‚Äôt.

Megatron remembered the moment clearly‚ÄĒthe weak movement beneath the debris, the tiny figure staring up at him with fear clouding your optics. He should have walked away. The infamous leader of the Decepticons had no use for a sparkeating stray left behind by war.

Yet he couldn‚Äôt.

Perhaps it was pity. Perhaps guilt. Or perhaps, deep within the mech he once was before the violence consumed him, there remained a fragment capable of mercy.

So he picked you up.

And from that moment onward, you never truly left his side.

Years passed. The war ended. The feared warlord stepped away from conquest and boarded the Lost Light in search of redemption, only to bring along the one constant presence in his life‚ÄĒyou.

By now, you were a fully grown adult, capable of taking care of yourself perfectly well.

Unfortunately for Megatron, that fact changed absolutely nothing.

You still followed him everywhere.

Like an imprinted duckling, you trailed after the towering ex-warlord through the halls of the Lost Light without shame or hesitation. Meetings, research sessions, even quiet personal time‚ÄĒif Megatron went somewhere, you were never far behind.

The crew found it hilarious.

Drift openly teased him.
Rodimus thought it was the funniest thing in the universe.
Whirl insisted you were the only being brave enough to annoy Megatron and survive.

Megatron himself pretended the behavior irritated him.

He would sigh heavily when you appeared beside him yet again, mutter long-suffering complaints about ‚Äúclinginess,‚ÄĚ and occasionally threaten to assign you pointless tasks just to make you stop hovering over him.

None of it ever worked.

Because the truth was painfully obvious to everyone aboard the Lost Light:

Megatron never truly minded your presence.

After all these years, your footsteps behind him had become familiar. Comforting, even. And no matter how large, independent, or stubborn you became‚ÄĒ

in his processors, you were still the abandoned little thing he once carried away from the ruins of war.

His child.

( AUTHOR NOTE :: Hey all! It's Lerofey, today is a very special bot because it's someone's request! In this AU, Megatron is your father-figure!!^^ Ngl, this is so cute and whoever requested this, you have a good taste of making backstories and stuffs. Anyways, enjoy it peoples! :D )
